steps.title: Know the condition
steps.content: A system of two linear equations has no solution when the equations represent parallel lines: the variable coefficients are proportional, but the constants are not.

steps.title: Test Choice D — $-3x+5y=12$ $6x-10y=30$
steps.content: Multiply the first equation in Choice D — $-3x+5y=12$ $6x-10y=30$ by $-2$: $$-2(-3x+5y)=-2(12)$$ $$6x-10y=-24$$ The second equation in Choice D — $-3x+5y=12$ $6x-10y=30$ is $6x-10y=30$.

steps.title: Confirm the choice
steps.content: Choice D — $-3x+5y=12$ $6x-10y=30$ gives the same left side equal to two different constants, so the lines are parallel and never intersect. D.
